Important: Please Save Your Own Copy Before Editing :) 



SIX FIGURE EMAILS # 6 

The “Anthropomorphic Activator (or Agitator)”



Ry Schwartz & Copyhackers

2020



SIX FIGURE EMAIL # 6

About This Template:

Background

Why we use it

What we’re “selling”:

Where to Use it:

How to Use It:

What can be Personified?

Exploratory Questions

Template 1 (As a Closing Sales Email)

Template 2 (As a Cart Abandonment)

See it in action:

Example 1: The Pissed Off Landing Page

Example 2 (Cart Abandonment)



About This Template: 

Some of the most popular (and persuasive) characters of all-time, weren’t humans, but anthropomorphized animals (ie. Tony the Tiger ), objects (ie. Toy Story), and feelings (“Inside Out”) 



What is it about these anthropomorphized “things” that draw us in closer, have us root harder, and want to jump into the screen to help them complete their mission or alleviate their pain?



And more importantly… is there an opportunity to leverage those same persuasion triggers in our own marketing?  



To let an “object” do the dirty work when our own appeals have failed to make the sale? 



Background



In October 2017, Copy Hackers launched 10X Landing Pages.



In the final day, we sent out a rather “unconventional” email.



It polarized the audience, resulting in a 4X “reply rate”... instantly splitting the field between the haters and the advocates. 



It was also one of the most effective single emails of the entire campaign: 



Lance Jones wrote a brilliant blog post breaking down some of the replies.



Since then, many people have reached out to dive deeper into the psychology of WHY this works, and how to apply it. 



Read Lance’s Epic Breakdown 

Why we use it



During an intensive email marketing campaign (ie. a launch when we’re mailing out every day… sometimes MULTIPLE times per day)... our audience will start losing a bit of receptivity to our message.



The Anthropomorphic Activator catalyzes action by being a major pattern interrupt, and demonstrating the problem from a new POV, while triggering some powerful persuasion triggers in the process. 



Change of Pace During Intensive Launch Period: Gives your audience a brief reprieve from the main sender, while STILL connecting them to the sales message. 

Acts as a pattern interrupt: Unexpected and unusual things grab our attention and hold it long enough to… 

See the problem/stakes/outcomes/benefits from a new angle. Sometimes we need a shift in perspective before the message finally resonates and compels us to act. This also allows us to “call out” our prospect on their resistance/procrastination in a less direct way. 

Defending the defenseless: When we see that the animal or object can’t fend for itself, it ignites the superman syndrome. The problem becomes bigger than ourselves. 



What we’re “coaching”:



Intimacy with the stakes/outcomes (made more intimate by animating the “thing”) 



Responsibility: We’re not responsible for more than just ourselves. (ie. the man who resists life insurance until he has a kid)



Urgency (as a result of becoming more intimately aware of the consequences/stakes) 



Empowered to act: In relation to the proxy (an animal, or thing), we appreciate our ability to act. 

Where to Use it:

This email would typically be sent in the final 24 hours of a promotion. By this point, they’ve already seen the offer and major sales arguments from you -- and therefore the shift in perspective or unique lens will have the greatest impact. 



Physical Goods / Ecommerce: : This template is also effective as a “cart abandonment” if you anthropomorphize the product being left in the cart. Poor thing. All alone and scared :( 



How to Use It: 

What can be Personified? 

Things

Your marriage certificate: I’ve sat on the mantle - watching you argue. Don’t you remember how you were when you signed me? 

Your financial statements “you ignore me” do something before the IRS kidnaps me, throws me in a black van, and pricks and probes me to see where we’ve messed up.

Feelings (ie. Inside Out) 

Objects

Your Car (ie. “I’m rusting prematurely”) 

Your House (“I’m vulnerable to break-ins)

Animals

Your Product

Their Pain (or the thing that experiences it)

Ie. Their hard drive (for anti virus software): “I’m vulnerable”

A Future Outcome (or the thing that experiences it) 

Ie. “Financial Independence” - I’m not as far as you think 

Your Dream Business - I’m not getting any younger:

You’ve had me on the backburner for 3 years waiting for the perfect time. Well here’s the thing. These crow’s feet are setting in, and I’m going more and more grey with each shiny object you chase. Soon I’ll be 6 feet under ground… 

The problem or pain your product helps solve 

Ie. “Your Love Handles” - “I’m ready” I’m ready to say goodbye. We’ve had some good times. Bonded over s'mores, and played in a ball pit of gluten and carbs. 

Your Menstrual Cramp

Other:

Your Style

Your Confidence

Your Future Partner

Your Future Self

Exploratory Questions



What “thing” experiences the pain/pleasure/gain/loss associated with the buying decision? 



Of those “things”, which would your audience be most sympathetic towards? Which is least able to fend for itself? 



In what ways is it affected? 



How do those pains relate back to your prospect? 



How has it felt neglected? Unappreciated? Underserved? 



What band-aid solutions can the “thing” call your prospect out on? 



What would life be like for both “the thing” and your prospect if the problem was fixed? 



Template 1 (As a Closing Sales Email) 



[Introduce yourself as “the thing”] 

[Drastic times call for drastic measures - WHY you’re emailing them + agitate the pain in a hyper-specific way] 

[Dramatize the pains you’ve been experiencing] 

[Gently (or crudely) call them out on their inaction or band-aid solutions] 

[List the benefits/outcomes you COULD be experiencing] 

[CTA: Make the close about “making things right” between them and the object]  

[OPTIONAL: Give the urgency/stakes] 

[CTA2] 

Template 2 (As a Cart Abandonment) 



[Introduce yourself as “the thing”] 

[Call of Distress] 

[What you’re really meant for - benefits]  

[biggest pain or challenge you help them overcome] 

[agitate YOUR pain as the “thing”]  

[CTA 1]  

[Optional Bribe]

[CTA 2]  



See it in action: 

Example 1: The Pissed Off Landing Page

Subject:

I’m not okay. 



Body: 



Hey.

It’s your landing page here.

ie. the forgotten, red-headed stepchild of your marketing funnel.

ie. the thing you send THOUSANDS of hard-earned, expensive clicks to, yet haven’t checked in for months to see how I was even doing.

(hint: I’m NOT ok)

Anyway, I know it’s weird that I’m reaching out to you like this.

But you left me no choice.

Cause we need to talk…

… and you’ve been ignoring my passive-aggressive cries for help for far too long.

Y’know, like how I've been…

… giving you cringe-worthy “your CMO will kill you” non-ROI on your ad spend.

… scaring away your visitors (and making sure they never return).

… making potential buyers bounce faster than an overcaffeinated kangaroo.

I thought that by doing all these things, you’d finally pay attention to me.

But nooooo…

Instead, you’ve paid even MORE attention to your top-of-funnel traffic strategies - those bloody ebooks nobody downloads cuz they have to read ME to download 'em, and I'm sure as hell not gonna convince them. As far as I'm concerned, no one should download all that free content you're PDFing like you just discovered PDFs.

So while you’ve been pulling together that next webinar or doubling down on Facebook ads…

.... I’ve been sulking here, playing Bob Dylan’s “It’s ok Ma, I’m only bleedin’” on an infinite loop.

I mean… WTF!?

What does a landing page have to do to get a little love around here?

And it wouldn’t make me so freakin’, well, angry, if it wasn’t for all the revenue we're just leaving on the table!

I mean, I exist to place revenue-generating leads AT YOUR FEET.

I exist to get people to sign up for YOUR webinars and buy YOUR products.

That's literally all I'm here for.

(yes, i'm still here)

Cause did you know that, with just a BIT of strategic tweaking (the kind the lady here can make really easy for you), we could easily:

Capture 2X, 5X or even 10X the clicks you’ve been tossing my way

Convert a whole bunch more leads into real live customers

Take a medieval mace to the face of our biggest competitors… while disrupting industries and creating new SaaS empires overnight (just gimme the chance before you look at me that way!)

Give you the confidence and cashflow to splurge on all those other marketing children you love so much - like that smug jack in Facebook ads, and don't get me started on the $5K you spent on Jim the Ebook, which was supposed to make you the next Marketo #imbetterthanjim #marketoinvestsinme



Look. I’m not asking for much.

I’m very forgiving.

Won't even hold a grudge.

But you need to meet me halfway.

Remember when you left me open on one of your Chrome tabs, intending to optimize me, before choosing to watch “Jerry Maguire” on Netflix instead?

Ya. Well I was watching too.

At one point, he said something along the lines of, “help me help you.”

Well, I remembered that line, thinking it would be handy some day.

So here we are.

I’m playing the “Jerry Card.”

So what do you say?

Help me help you

Joanna and her Copy Hackers gang are literally hours away from locking you and me out of their 10xLanding Pages copywriting course

Missing this would really suck.

Cause I need this. Okay? I need this.

WE need this.

And it’s about damn time we committed ourselves to a more profitable (and loving) marketing future together.

We can come back from this dark history of ours.

We can find landing page love again.

But only if you show me you care by 9 tonight

Sincerely,

Landing Page

PS. While I have you here, wow, it's been nice reconnecting. All of this time we've spent apart - I almost forgot how wonderful you and I are together. Now, okay, I know you've got Facebook ads you just have to get to, but let's put another date on the books, okay? Two, actually.

10/11 at 9am PST

10/25 at 9am PST

Joanna calls them “landing page clinics.”

I call them a *can't miss* chance to seriously 10x you and me

Yes, you’re gonna take a scalpel to my pixels and do some ungodly stuff.

But I’m willing to go through the pain IF it means higher conversions and more sales. If you're in, I'm in.

You’re getting the easier part of the deal here.

I get surgically ripped apart while you cash in?

No brainer. For you.

These clinics are included in 10x Landing Pages, no matter what plan you choose.

So like I mentioned:

It's a total no brainer - but it closes in:





Example 2 (Cart Abandonment) 



Subjects

Abandoned :( Reward: ___ 



Body: 



Hey. 



A few mins ago you were ready to take me home.



And now I sit here in this dank pixelized cart, wondering what the f*ck just happened. 



I’m cold.



Confused.



No idea what I’m doing in this sh*tty cart, when I should be with you… 



…  Giving you Benefit 1

…. Benefit 2

…. Benefit 3



While freeing you from [biggest pain] 



I’m destined for more than this. And so are you. 



<Author Name> and the rest of the <company name> crew have turned off the lights and gone home for the day. 



Probably to throw back some tasty merlot and play a round of Scrabble with the fam-jam. 



So what’d you say??



Invite me into yours? 



We’ll skip the merlot and get straight to <the action that leads to the benefit> 



Not like you should need it, but if this is what it takes… 



Here’s a 10% Good Samaritan Reward: 



Code: “FREE<ProductName>” 



<sign off>
